title: Braised Eggplant Parmesan

Produce: 3 medium Japanese eggplants (about 1¾ lbs. total), tops trimmed and halved lengthwise
¼ cup lightly packed fresh basil leaves, torn
Cheese: 1½ oz. low-moisture, part-skim mozzarella cheese, torn into ¼-in. pieces
1½ oz. Parmesan cheese, grated (about ¾ cup)

steps: Preheat oven to broil with a rack in center position. Heat a large cast-iron or broiler-proof stainless steel skillet over medium high until barely smoking. Sprinkle cut sides of eggplant with ½ tsp. of the salt. Add 3 Tbsp. of the olive oil to skillet and swirl to coat. Quickly arrange the eggplant cut-side down in skillet. Use a spatula to firmly press eggplant down; cook, undisturbed, until lightly charred and starting to soften, about 5 minutes.

Remove skillet from heat and transfer eggplant to a plate; set aside. Add remaining 2 Tbsp. oil to skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and crushed red pepper; cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and water to skillet; cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, pressing with the back of a spatula to burst them, 4 to 6 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining ½ tsp. salt.

Return eggplant to skillet; cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 minutes. Add more water if skillet looks dry before eggplant is tender. Taste sauce, and add salt if needed. Remove from heat.
Dot top of eggplant with mozzarella, and sprinkle with Parmesan. Transfer to oven, and broil until cheese melts and browns, 2 to 3 minutes. Top with basil; serve immediately.
